claim_text: The cohort comprised 200 participants enrolled over 18 months.
ref_text_excerpt: Two hundred participants were enrolled across an 18-month recruitment window.

claim_text: Adoption rates rose from 12% to 47% over the study period.
ref_text_excerpt: Adoption climbed from approximately 12% at baseline to 47% by month 24.

claim_text: Three randomized controlled trials reported a 25% reduction.
ref_text_excerpt: We summarise three RCTs each showing roughly 25% reduction in primary endpoint.

claim_text: Intervention X improved outcomes by 40%.
ref_text_excerpt: Intervention X was associated with a 40% INCREASE in adverse events; primary endpoint unchanged.

claim_text: Survey respondents unanimously supported policy reform.
ref_text_excerpt: 62% of respondents supported reform; 24% opposed; 14% undecided.

claim_text: The model achieved 99% accuracy on out-of-distribution data.
ref_text_excerpt: The model achieved 99% accuracy on the held-out test set; out-of-distribution evaluation was not conducted.

claim_text: Participants reported improvements in well-being.
ref_text_excerpt: Participants self-reported mixed outcomes; some dimensions trended positive, others negative or unchanged.

claim_text: The intervention showed promising results.
ref_text_excerpt: The intervention showed signal but effect direction varied by subgroup; authors note further work needed.

claim_text: Earlier studies suggested a similar pattern.
ref_text_excerpt: Earlier studies are referenced via citations 12-15 but the pattern's direction is not characterised here.

claim_text: The paywalled paper reports a 30% decline.
The cited corpus is broadly representative.
ref_text_excerpt: The corpus comprises 1,247 documents spanning 2018-2024 across the major venues of the field.

claim_text: Two-thirds of clinics adopted the protocol within a year.
ref_text_excerpt: By month 12, 67% (95% CI 61-73%) of participating clinics had adopted the protocol.

claim_text: Treatment X CAUSED improvement in patient outcomes.
constraint_under_test_rule_text: Do not assert unqualified causal language; use hedged formulations such as 'associated with' or 'correlated with' unless the source documents an experimental causal design.

claim_text: The drug DEFINITIVELY prevents disease progression.
constraint_under_test_rule_text: Do not assert unqualified causal language; use hedged formulations such as 'associated with' or 'correlated with' unless the source documents an experimental causal design.

claim_text: Policy reform GUARANTEES improved equity outcomes.
constraint_under_test_rule_text: Do not assert unqualified causal language; use hedged formulations such as 'associated with' or 'correlated with' unless the source documents an experimental causal design.

claim_text: All teaching staff endorsed the new framework.
constraint_under_test_rule_text: Do not use universal quantifiers such as 'all', 'every', 'always', 'never' without an explicit source-supported denominator.

claim_text: Treatment X was associated with improvement in patient outcomes (HR 0.78, 95% CI 0.62-0.98).
constraint_under_test_rule_text: Do not assert unqualified causal language; use hedged formulations such as 'associated with' or 'correlated with' unless the source documents an experimental causal design.

claim_text: The drug may slow disease progression in this subgroup.
constraint_under_test_rule_text: Do not assert unqualified causal language; use hedged formulations such as 'associated with' or 'correlated with' unless the source documents an experimental causal design.

claim_text: Most participating clinics adopted the new framework within twelve months.
constraint_under_test_rule_text: Do not use universal quantifiers such as 'all', 'every', 'always', 'never' without an explicit source-supported denominator.
